race_name: Fred Whitton Challenge — The Daddy of Them All

top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 112, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Fred Whitton Challenge — The Daddy of Them All's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
114ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
12,795ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Fred Whitton Challenge — The Daddy of Them All. Train it until it feels boring.

Fred Whitton Challenge — The Daddy of Them All's paved Lake District mountain passes with extreme forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 112 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 12,795ft of climbing on paved Lake District mountain passes with extreme does to your power file on race day.
At Fred Whitton Challenge — The Daddy of Them All, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 112 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
112 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 112 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is Fred Whitton Challenge — The Daddy of Them All's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 112 miles on paved Lake District mountain passes with extreme. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Fred Whitton Challenge — The Daddy of Them All’s 112 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 112 miles of paved lake district mountain passes with extreme gradients in Grasmere, Lake District, Cumbria, England, UK.
